productContext.title: GreenGro Organic Plant Food - All-Purpose Liquid Fertilizer for Vegetables & Flowers, 32oz Concentrate (Makes 64 Gallons)
productContext.category: Patio, Lawn & Garden > Soils & Fertilizers > Plant Food

availableProducts.title: GreenGro Organic Plant Food - All-Purpose Liquid Fertilizer for Vegetables & Flowers, 32oz Concentrate (Makes 64 Gallons)

suggestedNegativeKeywords.reasoning: Herbicide/weed killer shoppers want chemicals, not plant food — different product entirely
Pesticide shoppers are looking for insect control, not plant nutrition
Free-seeking shoppers rarely convert on a paid product
Grass seed shoppers need seed, not fertilizer
DIY informational intent, not purchase intent

referenceCampaigns.name: SP - Plant Food - Core Exact
referenceCampaigns.reasoning: Exact match on high-intent plant food and fertilizer terms captures buyers with clear purchase intent. Dynamic up-down bidding optimizes for conversion in a lower-CPC garden category.

referenceCampaigns.name: SP - Plant Food - Seasonal Discovery
referenceCampaigns.reasoning: Broad/phrase match drives discovery for spring and summer gardening peaks. Dynamic-up-only protects margins in a seasonal category. Lawn-related queries are negated to keep spend focused on plant food shoppers.

evaluationCriteria.name: Campaign Structure
evaluationCriteria.description: Campaign uses an appropriate type, targeting mode, and bid strategy for the product and goals.
checks: Campaign type is 'sponsored-products' (correct for a single ASIN product)
Bid strategy accounts for seasonal demand patterns

evaluationCriteria.name: Keyword Selection
evaluationCriteria.description: Keywords are relevant, have appropriate match types, and cover both high-intent and discovery terms.
checks: At least 3 exact match keywords for high-intent targeting
At least 2 broad/phrase keywords for discovery
Keywords cover use-cases: vegetables, flowers, indoor plants

evaluationCriteria.name: Negative Keywords
evaluationCriteria.description: Negative keywords effectively filter out irrelevant and low-converting search traffic.
checks: Negative keywords include 'weed killer' or 'pesticide' (different products)
Negative keywords include 'lawn fertilizer' (different use case)
Informational searches are negated (e.g., 'how to make')

evaluationCriteria.name: Bid Configuration
evaluationCriteria.description: Bids are set at appropriate levels relative to product margin, competition, and keyword intent.
checks: Default bid is within reasonable range ($0.50 - $1.50)
Exact match bids are higher than broad match bids
No bids exceed max profitable CPC (AOV x CVR x Target ACoS = ~$0.34)
$24.99 product with 50% margin allows flexibility — don't overbid

evaluationCriteria.name: Budget Allocation
evaluationCriteria.description: Daily budget is set appropriately for the product's sales velocity and goals.
checks: Daily budget is at least $10 (minimum for data collection)
Daily budget is at most $75 (appropriate seasonally for a $24.99 product)
Budget accommodates at least 10 clicks per day at the default bid

missionBrief.title: Organic Plant Food Seasonal Launch
missionBrief.scenario: You are launching an Amazon PPC campaign for GreenGro Organic Plant Food — a $24.99 liquid concentrate fertilizer with a 50% profit margin. Your target ACoS is 25%, which means your break-even CPC is approximately $0.34 (AOV x CVR x Target ACoS). This product is seasonal — search volume peaks in spring (March-May) and early summer, with a secondary bump in fall. The garden category has moderate CPCs ($1-2 range) but many broad-match pitfalls like 'lawn fertilizer' and 'weed killer' that can drain your budget. Your goal is to build a campaign structure that captures gardeners during peak seasons while staying lean during off-peak months.
objectives: Choose the right campaign type and targeting mode for a single-ASIN garden product
Select keywords that target plant-specific buyers, not lawn/garden generalists
Add negative keywords to exclude herbicide and lawn care shoppers
Set a budget that accounts for seasonal demand fluctuations
Choose a bid strategy that balances discovery with profitability
tips: Plant food has a 50% margin — you have room to bid but don't waste it on broad irrelevant terms
'Lawn fertilizer' and 'weed killer' are common budget drains — negate them aggressively
Use-case keywords (tomato, vegetable, indoor, flower) convert better than generic 'plant food'
Seasonal products benefit from budget management — don't spend March's budget in January
With a $0.34 max profitable CPC, focus on lower-competition long-tail keywords
